title: alcea kurdica var. kurdica
intro: Alcea kurdica var. kurdica, also known as the Kurdish Hollyhock or Kurdish Alcea, is a plant species within the mallow family (Malvaceae). Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which have been attributed to the presence of flavonoids and alkaloids. The health benefits of Alcea kurdica var. kurdica include its ability to aid in wound healing, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory issues such as coughs and colds. The plant can be used in herbal preparations, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often made by infusing the leaves and flowers in hot water or a solvent.
